top10.introHtml: Top 10 best-buy convertibles near Media, PA — ranked for a fun, affordable second car. Condition and reliability weighted over mileage. All picks have clean title or no-accident history where filterable. As of 2026-07-02.

picks.whyGood: Cheapest real sports car in the batch at $7,799. The E36/7 Z3 is one of BMW's most reliable roadsters — 2.3L inline-six, rear-wheel drive, no IMS bearing risk. 82k miles is young for an early Z3. Classic looks, stiff chassis, pure open-air fun. Clean title, no accidents per Cars.com filter.
picks.whatToVerify: Cycle the soft top fully — Z3 tops are early-2000s vintage and seals dry-rot; check for water stains on carpet and floor mat corners. Inspect the rear-window zipper seam. Look for rust on the sill jacking points (common PA/NJ salt-belt failure). Pull the oil cap for mayo (coolant leak). Ask for timing chain maintenance history.
picks.bottomLine: Best bang-for-buck roadster in the search — Z3 2.3 at under $8k with 82k miles is a rare clean find.

picks.whyGood: Only 45,587 miles on a 2004 is extraordinary — less than 2,200 miles per year on average. The A4 Cabriolet with the 1.8T is a solid daily driver with all-season capability and a proper VW-group retractable soft top. Clean title, no accidents. 20 miles from Media.
picks.whatToVerify: Pull codes for the 1.8T (common: timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start, N75 boost solenoid, evap purge valve). Check the soft-top hydraulic pump and all four corner seals — 20-year-old tops can look fine but leak at the A-pillar. Inspect for rust on the convertible's structural sill reinforcement (heavier than sedan). Check for oil leaks at the cam cover and oil pan.
picks.bottomLine: 45k miles in 2025 on a 2004 Audi convertible is nearly unheard of — probably stored or a third car; worth a thorough PPI at this price.

picks.whyGood: A 2015 MINI with 55k miles for under $10k is genuinely good value — this is a third-generation F57 platform (far better electronics than R57 predecessor). Only 12 miles from Media. Clean title, no accidents. The Cooper-spec 1.5T three-cylinder is reliable and fuel-efficient.
picks.whatToVerify: Verify the power-folding top mechanism works through a full cycle — MINI tops occasionally need actuator service around 60-80k. Check for oil leaks at the valve cover (common on B38 engine). Ask if timing chain service has been done (recommended ~70k). Inspect water-drain channels at the top frame corners.
picks.bottomLine: Newest car on the list at under $10k, close to home, with a reliable modern drivetrain — excellent choice for a casual second car.

picks.whyGood: 2007 Cooper S Convertible with 65k miles at $8,452 — the supercharged R53-generation S makes 172hp and is genuinely fun to drive. 65k miles is low for the model year. Josie's Auto Sales typically services their inventory.
picks.whatToVerify: Supercharger coupler is the known R53 weak point — listen for belt squeal and check for oil mist around the charger coupling. Pull the top boot and check for fraying. Inspect the rear-seat area for water intrusion stains. Ask for recent alternator, starter, and A/C compressor history (common repair items at 65-80k).
picks.bottomLine: Turbocharged fun for $8,400 with only 65k miles — strong value among the MINI convertibles in this batch.

picks.whyGood: The Kappa-platform Solstice is a legitimately sporty roadster — rear-wheel drive, low curb weight, balanced handling. 80k miles at $9,098 is fair-deal pricing for a car that's just genuinely fun. Base model means fewer electronics to fail. Similar to Saturn Sky platform.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft top manually through several full cycles — the folding mechanism is trunk-space invasive, confirm trunk opens with top down. Check the Power Steering pump (hydraulic, occasional leak). Inspect for rust at the door sill and rockers — Kappa cars can rust on the top frame mounts. Ask if the front sway bar end-links and rear toe links have been serviced.
picks.bottomLine: Lightweight RWD roadster at under $10k — one of the few genuinely sporty American convertibles in this price range.

picks.whyGood: 2013 Audi A5 Cabriolet, Good Deal-rated, 78k miles at $10,399. The A5 2.0T is one of Audi's best-rounded droptops — proper four-seat capacity, electric top operates quickly, and the 2.0T TFSI is robust at this mileage. Premium Plus trim means heated seats, Bang & Olufsen audio, and rear camera.
picks.whatToVerify: Check for oil consumption (common 2.0T issue pre-2013 service bulletin — verify if updated piston rings were installed). Inspect electric top operation fully; A5 tops are more reliable than predecessors but can develop rattle around the header bow. Look at rear-window defroster grid for cracks. CarGurus 'Good Deal' badge means it's priced below market avg.
picks.bottomLine: Best premium convertible value in the batch — Good Deal-rated A5 with leather and full power kit under $10,400.

picks.whyGood: E93 328i Convertible, only 9 miles from Media, 80k miles at $10,900. The E93 generation is the last BMW to use a traditional soft top (3-series went retractable hardtop in 2014) — lighter, less to fail. The N52 straight-six engine is one of BMW's most bulletproof naturally-aspirated motors.
picks.whatToVerify: Check all four soft-top seals and the front corner drain tubes (classic E93 water-in-footwell failure). N52 valve cover gaskets and oil filter housing gasket are common leaks by 80k. Pull codes — VANOS actuator faults are frequent but inexpensive to clear. Verify the convertible top hydraulic pump has no whine.
picks.bottomLine: Closest BMW convertible to Media at 9 miles, bulletproof N52 six-cylinder, only 80k miles.

picks.whyGood: 177k miles would eliminate most cars — not the NC Miata. The MX-5's 2.0L naturally-aspirated engine routinely reaches 200-250k with basic maintenance. Grand Touring trim means Bose audio, leather, heated seats. At $10,950 for a 2012 Grand Touring, a healthy Miata at any mileage is worth a PPI.
picks.whatToVerify: NC Miata specific: check for timing chain rattle on cold start (chain guide wear), inspect the passenger floor for water intrusion (drain plug behind the seat), look at the soft-top rear window stitching for separation. Ask about timing chain replacement history. Verify power windows and power retractable hardtop (if PRHT variant) — confirm which variant this is.
picks.bottomLine: The only Miata in the batch — high mileage is fine for the platform; buy with a clean PPI and drive it to 250k.

picks.whyGood: Second Z3 in the list — slightly lower mileage (75k vs 82k) at $10,340 vs $7,799. More expensive than rank #1 but still excellent value for a Z3 with lower miles in Willow Grove. Both have clean title / no accidents per Cars.com filters.
picks.whatToVerify: Same Z3 checklist as rank #1: soft top seals and rear window seam, sill rust at jacking points, oil cap for mayo, timing chain history. At $10,340, negotiate if any deferred maintenance is found.
picks.bottomLine: Alternative Z3 at 75k miles — pay the extra $2,500 over rank #1 only if condition is demonstrably better.

picks.whyGood: 74k miles on a 2001 Boxster at $11,250 — the M96 engine's IMS bearing failure risk peaked 1997-1999; the 2001 MY uses a single-row bearing that is still a concern but statistically less frequent than earliest years. If this car has IMS documentation or a retrofit, it moves much higher on the list. Private seller often means better history knowledge.
picks.whatToVerify: IMS bearing is the critical question — ask seller directly if IMS retrofit was performed (LN Engineering or equivalent). Pull coolant reservoir for any oil contamination. Check for AOS (Air Oil Separator) failure — black oily residue around the exhaust. Inspect soft top rear window for delamination (common on 20+ year cars). Run VIN through Porsche service history if possible.
picks.bottomLine: Only buy with IMS documentation or budget $500-800 for a pre-purchase IMS inspection; if clean, a 74k Boxster at $11k is a steal.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles in budget near Media, PA — ranked GT/V8 first, then by mileage and proximity.

picks.whyGood: Only 44,000 miles on a 1994 GT Convertible is extraordinary — this is an SN-95 Fox-4 with the 5.0L V8 (215hp) or the final year of the pushrod small-block. 44k miles in 30 years means it was rarely driven. Clean title, no accidents per Cars.com filters. Bensalem is 18 miles away.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 convertible-specific: check the convertible top mount reinforcement at the top of the rear quarters (rust-prone on all Fox-body/SN-95 droptops). Inspect the rear frame rails under the trunk for rust perforation. Verify it's actually a GT (5.0L) vs V6 — check door jamb sticker. Top seals, rear window condition, and power-top mechanism are all 30-year-old items.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest-mileage Mustang convertible in the batch by far — 44k miles on a GT is a time capsule worth a serious look.

picks.whyGood: 1995 Mustang GT with 55k miles at $9,995 from GT Motors — also very low mileage for the model year. The 5.0L GT was in its final year before the 4.6L modular engine arrived in 1996. Purist collectors prefer the pushrod 5.0L for its aftermarket depth and tuning headroom.
picks.whatToVerify: Same SN-95 rust inspection as rank #1. Verify GT vs base — check VIN character 8 (should be 'D' for 5.0L HO). Inspect convertible top well for water pooling. Ask about recent rubber refreshes (top seals, weatherstripping). Automatic vs manual — confirm transmission type.
picks.bottomLine: Second low-mileage SN-95 GT in budget — the 5.0L pushrod V8 is a classic for a reason.

picks.whyGood: 2012 Mustang GT Convertible — 5.0L Coyote V8 (412hp), modern suspension, 9 miles from Media. 96k miles is completely manageable for a Coyote V8 which is known to go well past 200k. West Chester location means quick test drive. Fair Deal rating means pricing is close to market.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 convertible-specific: check rear seat bottom for water staining from soft-top seal leaks. Test power top through full cycle. Inspect top tonneau cover for tears. Listen for any tick from the Coyote V8 on cold start (normal vs. tick that persists indicates cam phaser or lifter wear). Rear axle clunk on acceleration/deceleration — common 8.8 diff lash issue.
picks.bottomLine: Most modern V8 Mustang convertible in budget and closest to home — Coyote power with 96k is a proven combination.

picks.whyGood: Fox-body 1987 GT Convertible — the definitive American muscle convertible of the 1980s. 5.0L HO V8, T-5 manual (if equipped), 98k miles at $9,500. Clean title, no accidents per Cars.com. A well-preserved Fox body is increasingly collectible and parts availability is phenomenal.
picks.whatToVerify: Fox-body convertibles have known structural flex — inspect the cowl area above the windshield for cracks, check the A-pillar corners for rust bubbling (water pools here). The manual top mechanism on 1987s requires a helper — test operation. Inspect the floor pans front and rear for rust-through (common in PA/NJ). Ask about timing chain and serpentine belt history on the 5.0L.
picks.bottomLine: Classic Fox-body GT at 98k miles — buy it for the experience, expect some vintage maintenance; parts are everywhere.

picks.whyGood: Newest Mustang in budget — S550 EcoBoost Convertible (2.3T, 310hp), 2018 with modern safety and infotainment. 141k miles is high but EcoBoost 2.3T Mustangs have proven durable if coolant/oil maintenance is kept up. At $12,992 this is the most feature-rich car.
picks.whatToVerify: EcoBoost-specific: check for coolant consumption and thermostat housing leaks (2.3T common issue on S550). Inspect power-retractable soft top for any motor whine or hesitation. Pull codes — EcoBoost turbos can develop boost leaks at intercooler couplings. 141k miles warrants a compression test and coolant analysis before purchase.
picks.bottomLine: Most modern Mustang convertible available but high miles — buy only with a clean compression test and recent coolant flush history.
